{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Bishop's Opening: Calabrese counter-gambit| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd| | |pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | |pd|pd| | |= | | |bl| |pl| | | |= | | | | | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl| |nl|rl|= || }} == Bishop's Opening - Calabrese Countergambit == === 2... f5?! === Black uses a risky attacking system to end up with a firm center, while white pieces struggle to develop great squares. White must play very precisely, or they can easily find themselves in a lot of trouble. White's main options: :* [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Bc4/2...f5/3. d3|3. d3]] Jaenisch variation. :* [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Bc4/2...f5/3. d4|3. d4]] :* 3. Nc3 :* 3. Nf3 ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. '''1. e4 e5 2.
